summary: Close swap (design-the-kit plan -> handoff-prompt generator) is pedagogically coherent as a capstone: meta-frame closer placement holds, student agency intact, the 'what this is not' carve-out blocks autonomous-agent overreach, the handoff prompt is a real forcing function producing a durable cold-runnable artifact, and the new LO/leap-test/artefact-contract align with what the lecture+prompt actually produce. Rule-15 sim+eval now re-fired: three-persona sim (senior / mid-training / CTO) + handoff-generator live-test ran. The live-test confirmed the generated handoff is cold-safe (six named shapes carried, on-disk session-shaper referenced as the worked example, repos re-studied, no placeholders, a runnable prompt not a meta-summary). Sim surfaced one mood line anthropomorphising the runtime at the Ralph beat; reshaped. Clear at sim-grade; a real-cohort live-test on an actual M6 close is still owed — no cohort has run it yet.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Meta-frame lectures are closers, not openers
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:67 placement block: 'Module 6 closer chain - Human close -> The loop has a name -> Agents that build agents -> Next.' line:3 'This lecture is the unfolding of that line.' Names the flywheel AFTER the student authored two skills (lines 5, 11) - recognition, not abstract claim. Module file line:61 lists it last in the Human-close chain. Correct closer placement.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Minimal lecturing before contrast-mood exercises
rules_evaluated.evidence: Terminal closer. No contrast-mood exercise follows in M6 or the core training; rule governs openers before M5-style contrast modules.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Walk-away test (M2+) - student leaves while agent works
rules_evaluated.evidence: Closer-shaped, but the new close honors the walk-away spirit: the handoff prompt is authored to be run LATER, cold, in a fresh session (prompt body: 'paste into a fresh session when I'm ready to grow my kit'; lecture line:33 'Save it where you will find it'). The student is not asked to read-and-paste-back in-seat. No backstop reading of Claude output for correctness (rule 30 also clean - the read at line 15-16 is taste/judgement on a draft prompt, not error-checking).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Trust the agentic design - no autonomous-agent overreach
rules_evaluated.evidence: The '## What this is not' section (lines 19-25) is the load-bearing carve-out: 'This is not the agent writing its own skills without you in the room ... Claude proposes, you steer' (line 21). 'Anything further pretends the agent has access it does not have' (line 25). The agent's authority is named (read artefacts on disk, draft the prompt); the student's judgement is named as the input the agent lacks (codebase knowledge, politics, roadmap - line 23). No overreach; the close defends explicitly against the autonomous-agent fantasy the maintainer mood-target flags (line 71).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Student agency - adults decide; opportunity != compulsion
rules_evaluated.evidence: '## A prompt to try' (line 27) frames the close as optional. line:17 'you decide whether it earns a place in your kit before you ever run it.' line:33 'Save it where you will find it. The kit you grow on your own is the one that counts.' No forced pacing, no compulsion. The artifact is run on the student's own schedule, cold.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Delegation boundaries - student brings, Claude screens
rules_evaluated.evidence: Boundary is correct: Claude reads the artefact it can reach (the shipped skill on disk, explicit path) and drafts; the student supplies the judgement Claude cannot - which repos, which shapes are worth packaging, which proposals earn a place (lines 15-17, 23). The handoff prompt instructs Claude to study the student's OWN repos (student brings the surface), conversation + push-back + invoke-to-verify (Claude screens/drafts). No instruction asking Claude to reach an inaccessible surface unaided.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Artifact-realism check - every named tool/skill/path exists and is available
rules_evaluated.evidence: The handoff prompt names '~/.claude/skills/session-shaper/SKILL.md' by explicit disk path; that skill is produced by the M6 exercise (exercise prompt-5 produces: second-authored-skill at ~/.claude/skills/<skill-name>/SKILL.md, exercise lines 84-89). It exists by M6 close by training design, so the prompt is cold-safe and does not depend on scrollback for the name (maintainer block line:83 confirms this is resolved). Prompt-graph requires (second-authored-skill from prompt:spot-gaps-build-the-loop-5) resolves against a real producer.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Teach the principle and the technique - default
rules_evaluated.evidence: Principle (WHY): the flywheel is collaboration, agent runs on disk-evidence and stops where judgement is the input (lines 21-25). Technique (HOW): the explicit handoff-prompt move - read the shipped skill, widen the move to the whole stack, study/diagram/author, conversation + verify (line 13, prompt body). Not a black-box invocation; the move the prompt drives is the same one the student just practiced, named and widened.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Auto-fire sim + eval after significant rewrite
rules_evaluated.evidence: Sim+eval re-fired 2026-05-31 after the close swap. Three-persona sim: senior reads the 'what this is not' carve-out as a real information-asymmetry distinction (evidence-on-disk vs evidence-in-head), not hedging; mid-training finds the handoff prompt runnable with a noted deferred-payoff caveat (the move builds a prompt run later, not in-session kit growth); CTO reads it as practitioner-shaped once the one vendor-anthropomorphism line was reshaped. Handoff-generator live-test on a constructed M6 close: the generated prompt is cold-safe - carries the six named shapes, points the cold session at the on-disk session-shaper as the worked example, instructs repo re-study, no placeholders, a real runnable prompt not a meta-summary. Quality dimension-log SHAs updated (writing/story/strategy/pedagogy @182969a).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose; forcing function vs scripted reaction
rules_evaluated.evidence: The forcing function lives in the fenced registry prompt {{prompt:agents-that-build-agents-handoff}} (line 31), not in body prose. It is a real forcing function, not a demo: it makes the agent perform a concrete move (read the shipped skill from an explicit disk path, author a standalone cold-runnable prompt) and produces a durable artifact the student carries past the training. The load-bearing constraint - 'has to stand on its own ... run it cold' - is in the prompt, enforcing self-containment. Body around it (lines 29, 33) is post-prompt follow-on guidance (read it as a draft, save it), not a forcing function in prose. Correctly layered.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cohort execution is trainer-paced + concurrent
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lecture, not a timed exercise. The close artifact is run by the student later, off-clock; no in-class execution budget to model.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Host-skill optional; maintainer block names trainer, not host-skill
rules_evaluated.evidence: No push-back moves authored in this lecture's maintainer block; push-backs live in the module/exercise files. No host-skill dependency.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Reflection beats - in-chat over file-write
rules_evaluated.evidence: The close produces a real downstream-consumed artifact (a handoff prompt the student runs later - artefact-contract row, module line 100), not a proof-of-reflection file-write. Student saves it where they choose (line 33); no mandated reflection file.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Classroom default - body teaches the work, no authored conversation cues
rules_evaluated.evidence: No 'share with the room / pair up / discuss / take N minutes' sentences. Body stays on the work (the move, the prompt to try, Ralph). No trainer-cue or pacing beats in body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Audience tier determines authoring substrate
rules_evaluated.evidence: AE101 = engineer ICs; the close produces a Claude Code prompt + reads a CLI skill at ~/.claude/skills/. Substrate matches the tier (skill files + paste-able prompt). The student can run it Day 1 without an install stealing the teaching moment - the skill is already shipped by M6.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Eval / judge architecture - sim/eval verdicts read-only
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lecture content does not build eval machinery; rule governs the eval system, not this artifact.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-exercise leap test - observable Monday outcomes (arc-mood: artifact + use)
rules_evaluated.evidence: Module leap-test bullet (spot-gaps-build-the-loop.md line 90): 'The student now holds a standalone handoff prompt, written by the agent from the shipped session-shaper, that studies their whole stack and authors a skill per recurring shape when run later. The encode-move generalised from one task to all their work.' Arc-mood carve-out satisfied (names ARTIFACT + USE, not activity). Falsifiable: a working engineer can tell whether they hold such a prompt and have run it. Coheres with what the lecture+prompt produce.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Artefact contracts - every produced artefact with a stable identifier gets a contract row
rules_evaluated.evidence: Module file line 100 adds the contract row: 'Workflow-skills handoff prompt | Standalone prompt in the close scrollback ... | agents-that-build-agents close - the agent reads the shipped session-shaper and writes a cold-runnable prompt | The student's own later sessions: run cold to study their stack, diagram recurring shapes, author a skill per shape.' Producer and consumer named correctly; matches the lecture close and the registry produces block (workflow-skills-handoff-prompt).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Heavy fan-out prompts get a body-side recovery callout
rules_evaluated.evidence: The handoff prompt produces a PROMPT (single authored artifact), not a fan-out run at close time - 'What comes back is a prompt, not a plan' (line 33). The heavy read-the-whole-stack fan-out is deferred to the LATER cold run of the produced prompt, off the M6 clock; no in-lecture fan-out needing a recovery callout. The body does steer reading the draft with judgement/push-back (lines 15-17), which covers the only in-seat activity.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Convenience-driven prompts with known shape-bias get body-side acknowledgment callouts
rules_evaluated.evidence: The single-paste convenience risk (self-charity on a self-drafted prompt) is acknowledged in body: the student is told to read the returned prompt as a draft and apply 'Judgement, push-back, taste. Some of it will be obvious. Some of it will be off' (lines 15-16). The convenience choice is named with the harsher-reading agency the rule asks for.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Pre-action framing doesn't preview the prompt's payoff
rules_evaluated.evidence: Pre-prompt framing (lines 29, 'Ask the agent to read the skill you authored and write you a standalone prompt that builds the rest') names the ACTIVITY and the terminal ARTIFACT (a standalone prompt), not what the prompt will reveal/surface. Per the rule's keep-axes (names activity / names artifact / names constraint = KEEP), this is artifact-contract framing, not payoff preview. No pre-action 'What happened' recap.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Engineer-audience audit fixes default to body callout, not forcing function
rules_evaluated.evidence: No audit-fix being applied in this grade; rule governs fix-shape selection during revision, not a property of the shipped artifact.
